rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PETER KONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Based on the fair market value of the real property at the time of the predeceased spouse's death ($1,160,000), the property value increased by $916,001 from the beginning loan balance ($243,999 when the property was last refinanced in 2011). Should this value increase be allocated 100% to community property, in light of the rationale described in Marriage of Marsden (1982) 130 Cal.App.3d 426, 438? 2) After the predeceased spouse's death (and until the real property was sold), the property value further increased by $446,000 (from $1,160,000). Should this additional value increase be apportioned between community property and separate property by applying the reverse Moore/Marsden formula, based on the percentage calculated by separate property contributions made by the decedent prior to his death ($20,400) + separate property contributions deemed to have been made from the commingled pot of the estate personal property (73% of $7,002.39 = $5,111.74) , divided by the 2011 loan amount ($243,999) as the adjusted purchase price? If so, should 94.89% of the additional value increase be allocated to community property and 5.11% to separate property? PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DAPHNE ELIZABETH REECE REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST, DATED MAY 8, 2020
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Transferring Deceased Settlor'S Assets Into Trust; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Petition
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/20/2023. If there is no personal representative of the settlor's probate estate, each heir of the settlor must be served in the manner required for a summons in a civil action under sections 851(b)(3) & 17203(b), absent a signed waiver of service. According to the supplement, all the heirs appear to be residents of the United Kingdom. As such, the service must be effected in compliance with the Hague Service Convention to which the United Kingdom is a party to. See CCP section 413.10(c).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE PERCY L. EARBY AND JACQUELYN J.G. EARBY 2007 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Orders To Prove Existence Of Trust, To Remove Respondent As Cotrustee, To Return Trust Assets, To Compel Accounting, Breach Of Fiduciary Duty And Surcharge, And Prevent Respondent From Using Trust Funds To Defend This Removal Petition
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Counsel to report the court with status of the case. Procedural issues: The caption sets forth order to compel accounting, breach of fiduciary duty and surcharge and prevent respondent from using trust funds to defend this removal petition; however, such requests are not prayed. Also, petition prays for reasonable costs and attorney's fees in the amount to be determined by the court; however, petition does not provide any relevant info.
